subject: Public Health
title: Iodine and Brain Development
description: Learn Iodine and Brain Development in one minute.

hook.text: A tiny trace mineral—iodine—is the single biggest preventable cause of brain damage worldwide.

buildup.text: Your thyroid needs iodine to make hormones that control metabolism and fetal brain development.

discovery.text: Iodine deficiency during pregnancy can cause cretinism—severe, irreversible intellectual disability.

twist.text: Adding iodine to salt—costing just cents per person per year—has prevented millions of cases of brain damage.

climax.text: Iodized salt is one of the most cost-effective public health interventions ever implemented.

punchline.text: A pinch of salt protects a lifetime of thought.

quiz.question: What organ needs iodine to produce essential hormones?
